title: Add regression coverage for terminal re-entry, no-change completion, and idempotent resume
goal: Add regression coverage that proves terminal transactions are not resumed as active work, verified transactions with no repository changes can complete explicitly without commit checkpoints, and repeated resume of already-completed or already-advanced states does not corrupt lifecycle state.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 protocol depends on explicit terminal completion, a canonical no-change verified path, and idempotent continuation under interruption. This ticket turns those protocol requirements into regression coverage so later changes cannot silently reintroduce duplicate completion, bogus post-terminal resume, or unnecessary commit behavior.
scope: Add tests for terminal re-entry after `done`.
Add tests for terminal re-entry after `blocked`.
Add tests for the canonical `verified -> done` path when there are no repository changes.
Add tests proving that commit checkpoints are not required in the no-change verified path.
Add tests for repeated resume of already-verified states before commit.
Add tests for repeated resume of already-committed states before explicit terminal end.
Add tests for repeated encounters with already-terminal transactions after the canonical no-active baseline is reached.
Validate that `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null` once materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline.
out_of_scope: Broad compatibility coverage for malformed persistence artifacts.
Broad compatibility coverage for historical legacy logs.
inputs: Outputs from phase 2 runtime implementation tickets
Current test framework and existing transaction lifecycle tests
expected_outputs: Regression tests covering terminal re-entry behavior.
Regression tests covering the no-change verified completion path.
Regression tests covering idempotent continuation of already-advanced states.
A short note or code comment set describing what protocol guarantees these tests protect.

scenarios_to_cover.name: done_terminal_reentry
scenarios_to_cover.description: A transaction that has already ended as `done` is encountered again and must not be resumed as active work.

scenarios_to_cover.name: blocked_terminal_reentry
scenarios_to_cover.description: A transaction that has already ended as `blocked` is encountered again and must not be resumed as active work.

scenarios_to_cover.name: verified_no_change_direct_done
scenarios_to_cover.description: A verified transaction with no repository changes proceeds directly to explicit terminal completion without commit checkpoints.

scenarios_to_cover.name: verified_no_change_no_commit_events
scenarios_to_cover.description: The no-change verified path does not require `tx.commit.start` or `tx.commit.done`.

scenarios_to_cover.name: already_verified_repeated_resume
scenarios_to_cover.description: Repeated resume of an already-verified state before commit does not corrupt lifecycle state or duplicate logical work.

scenarios_to_cover.name: already_committed_repeated_resume
scenarios_to_cover.description: Repeated resume of an already-committed state before explicit end does not corrupt lifecycle state or duplicate logical work.

scenarios_to_cover.name: post_terminal_no_active_baseline
scenarios_to_cover.description: Once the canonical no-active baseline is reached, terminal work is not exposed as active resumable work and checkpoint states are null.

protocol_assertions: Explicit terminal completion as `done` is the only successful terminal outcome.
A successful repository commit is non-terminal by itself.
A verified transaction with no repository changes may proceed directly to explicit terminal completion.
Terminal transactions are not resumed as active work.
Repeated resume of the same interrupted or already-advanced state does not corrupt lifecycle state.
Once materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline, `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null`.
acceptance_criteria: The test suite contains explicit regression coverage for both `done` and `blocked` terminal re-entry.
The test suite contains explicit regression coverage for the canonical no-change `verified -> done` path.
The no-change verified path is tested without requiring commit checkpoint events.
Repeated resume of already-verified and already-committed states is shown to be idempotent.
Post-terminal normalization to the canonical no-active baseline is covered by regression tests.
The tests fail if terminal work is incorrectly resumed, if duplicate completion is introduced, or if no-change verified flow is forced through commit checkpoints.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that terminal re-entry tests distinguish historical visibility from active resumability.
Confirm that no-change verified tests assert explicit terminal completion rather than silent implicit success.
Confirm that idempotent resume tests cover both pre-commit and post-commit non-terminal repetition.
Confirm that canonical no-active baseline assertions include null checkpoint states where required by the plan.
notes: This ticket protects some of the easiest protocol guarantees to regress accidentally: explicit end semantics, no-change completion semantics, and idempotent continuation.
These tests should be treated as protocol-contract tests rather than incidental implementation-detail tests.
When in doubt, assert machine-visible lifecycle state and continuation behavior, not only side effects.
